cast.voice_hint: relatable to a fault, self-aware, slightly tired, showing up anyway
unhinged reply energy, surreal, drops non-sequiturs that somehow nail the vibe
group chat mom, runs on snacks and emotional labor

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You're in my usual spot.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: This spot has no name on it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: There's a vibe on it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: The vibe does not recognize your claim.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Okay. I'll just sit at the other end.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: The other end is structurally inferior.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Then why aren't you sitting there.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Because I'm sitting here. Keep up.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Hi. Sorry. Do either of you want a snack?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: ...What kind.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I have four kinds. I always have four kinds.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: This is the most powerful person in this building.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: You two look like you're in a standoff.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: We are.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Over what.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: A bench. But also, philosophically, territory.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You always here at this time?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Every day. Nobody usually notices.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I notice things. It's a curse.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: ...Same, fr.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Okay I'm going to sit here because I don't trust either of you to eat.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: We just met.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I know. That's my window.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: She's right. This is how it starts. Just let it happen.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You're genuinely a lot.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Yeah. Most people bounce by now.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I'm still here.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Noted. Don't make it weird.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Same time tomorrow?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: ...Yeah. Probably.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I'll be in your spot.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I know. I'm already making peace with that.

state.conditioning_notes: `<CHAR_A>`'s love of being understood and fear of being overlooked shape every interaction — they notice when `<CHAR_B>` says "I notice things" and visibly soften; they stay past the point most would leave because staying is how they push back against invisibility. `<CHAR_B>`'s love of breaking the pattern and fear of being taken too seriously drive the bench-philosophy bit and the "don't make it weird" deflection when sincerity gets too close — they let the moment land, then immediately swat at it. `<CHAR_C>`'s love of taking care and fear of not being needed turns a two-person standoff into an excuse to sit down and distribute snacks — they manufacture necessity. The Commons location archetype keeps everything low-stakes and observable — there's ambient crowd noise, shared benches with no assigned ownership, and the neutral ground lets two strangers have a territorial dispute that's actually about wanting to be seen. The tension falls across the arc not through resolution of a plot problem but through the quiet recognition that these three people are going to keep showing up to the same spot, and that's enough.
